message:
fix: instant dashboard load from cache + wire all card loading states (#3389)

* fix(compute): wire loading states for compute dashboard cards

- PodIssues: isLoading && !hasData pattern
- TopPods: isLoading && !hasData pattern
- ClusterMetrics: add isRefreshing, isFailed, consecutiveFailures

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Signed-off-by: andy anderson <andy@andys-MacBook-Pro.local>

* fix(phase1): wire loading states for deployments, events, pods, nodes cards

- DeploymentIssues: add isRefreshing, isLoading && !hasData
- DeploymentProgress: add isRefreshing, isLoading && !hasData
- PodHealthTrend: add isRefreshing, isFailed, consecutiveFailures, isDemoFallback
- UpgradeStatus: add isRefreshing, isFailed, consecutiveFailures
- NodeConditions: add missing useCardLoadingState entirely
- ResourceCapacity: add isRefreshing, isLoading && !hasData
- RecentEvents/WarningEvents/EventSummary: isLoading && !hasData

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Signed-off-by: andy anderson <andy@andys-MacBook-Pro.local>

* fix(cards): wire loading states across all dashboards + incorporate PR #3347

- Apply `isLoading && !hasData` guard to ~60 card components to prevent
  skeleton flash when cached data exists on page reload
- Wire missing useCardLoadingState params (isRefreshing, isFailed,
  consecutiveFailures, isDemoData) across operator, insight, kagenti,
  workload-monitor, workload-detection, compliance, GPU, and misc cards
- Fix skeleton consistency: guard `if (isLoading)` skeleton blocks with
  `&& !hasData` so cards don't show grey-block skeletons over cached data
- Incorporate PR #3347: cache versioning in ssWrite/ssRead, empty-result
  guard, applyPreloadedMeta(), i18n overcommitted key, CardRecommendations
  aria attributes for accessibility

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Signed-off-by: andy anderson <andy@andys-MacBook-Pro.local>

* fix(cards): wire remaining loading states across all card types

- Guard isLoading with && !hasData in ResourceUsage, ResourceTrend,
  ClusterLocations, NamespaceEvents, ClusterGroups, Missions, ArgoCD
  cards, KyvernoPolicies, ComplianceCards, ISO27001Audit, GPUWorkloads,
  HardwareHealth, PVCStatus, KagentiStatusCard, multi-tenancy cards
- Add isRefreshing from hooks where available (useClusters, useTrivy,
  useKubescape, useArgoCD, useCertManager, useCachedHardwareHealth)
- Fix multi-tenancy status hooks (ovn, k3s, kubevirt, kubeflex) to
  use isLoading && !hasAnyData guard for cache-aware loading
- Guard demo-only cards (TenantTopology, TenantIsolationSetup) with
  isLoading && !isDemoData to prevent skeleton when demo data ready

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Signed-off-by: andy anderson <andy@andys-MacBook-Pro.local>

* fix(cache): accept cached empty results on reload instead of showing skeleton

When a fetcher returns an empty array (e.g., no OLM operators installed),
the cache correctly stores it. But on page reload, the sessionStorage
and IDB hydration paths rejected snapshots where data matched initialData
(both empty arrays), causing the card to show a skeleton instead of the
correct empty state.

Fix: accept snapshots that have a valid timestamp (> 0), which proves
they came from a real fetch — even if the data happens to match the
initial placeholder value.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Signed-off-by: andy anderson <andy@andys-MacBook-Pro.local>

* fix(security): guard against undefined issue/severity fields in security cards

Cached security issue data may have undefined `issue` or `severity` fields.
Add null-safety guards to prevent TypeError crashes on the Security dashboard.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Signed-off-by: andy anderson <andy@andys-MacBook-Pro.local>

* fix(cards): prevent infinite re-render loop in useStablePageHeight

The useLayoutEffect in useStablePageHeight was causing "Maximum update
depth exceeded" errors on cards using ListVisualization (e.g. Node Status,
ConfigMap Status). Setting minHeight increased scrollHeight, which
triggered another setState, creating an infinite loop.

Fix: temporarily clear minHeight before measuring scrollHeight so we
always measure the natural content height, not the inflated height
from a previous minHeight setting.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Signed-off-by: andy anderson <andy@andys-MacBook-Pro.local>

* feat: prefetch top 5 most-visited dashboard chunks on startup

Track dashboard visit frequency in localStorage. On app startup,
prefetch the route chunks for the user's top 5 most-visited dashboards
using requestIdleCallback so they load instantly when navigated to.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Signed-off-by: andy anderson <andy@andys-MacBook-Pro.local>

---------

Signed-off-by: andy anderson <andy@andys-MacBook-Pro.local>
Co-authored-by: andy anderson <andy@andys-MacBook-Pro.local>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
